scope: `src/agents/research-agent/*` and the `chat-turn` entry point at `src/api/chat/route.ts`. Out of scope: the offline summarization job at `scripts/digest.ts`.
data_sources: Static code analysis. No live Langfuse data — project has no observability wired yet.
summary: Northcrop's research agent is a Vercel AI SDK loop that exposes 41 tools to the model on every turn through `src/agents/research-agent/tools.ts:18`. The top-level shape is sound — a single named agent, clean entry point, no recursion — but the **tool surface is the dominant cost and quality risk** in the codebase today: descriptions are long, several pairs are near-duplicates, and there is no pre-filtering. There is **no observability wired anywhere**, which means none of the cost or quality claims in this report are independently verifiable today; closing that loop is the highest-leverage next step. The eval suite under `evals/` is real and runs on PRs — the strongest dimension in the assessment.

dimensions.name: Agent topology
dimensions.label: Strong
dimensions.why: A single named agent with a clean entry point and no recursion or hidden sub-agent fan-out; responsibilities are clearly separated. Only a minor deduction because the summarizer is invoked inline rather than as a declared step.

dimensions.name: Tool surface
dimensions.label: Weak
dimensions.why: All 41 tools are passed on every turn with no pre-filtering, descriptions average 187 tokens, and three pairs are near-duplicates — the dominant cost and tool-selection risk. See the three Tool surface findings below.

dimensions.name: Context management
dimensions.label: Adequate
dimensions.why: Prompt and conversation assembly is sane, but the system prompt is an inline template with no version constant, so prompt regressions will be invisible once tracing lands.

dimensions.name: Decomposition
dimensions.label: Adequate
dimensions.why: Work is reasonably split — a dedicated summarizer step exists — but the research→summarise→cite→synthesize runbook is duplicated across three call sites rather than factored into one unit.

dimensions.name: Model routing
dimensions.label: Adequate
dimensions.why: A single capable model handles every step; fine for now, but no cheaper model is used for the mechanical summarizer step, where it would cut cost with no quality loss.

dimensions.name: Error handling
dimensions.label: Adequate
dimensions.why: Tool calls are wrapped and failures surface to the loop, but there is no retry/backoff on transient tool errors and malformed-output handling leans on SDK defaults.

dimensions.name: Observability
dimensions.label: Missing
dimensions.why: No tracing of any kind — no Langfuse/OTel/Helicone packages, no telemetry init, no env vars. The agent runs in production completely unobserved. This is the Critical finding.

dimensions.name: Cost discipline
dimensions.label: Weak
dimensions.why: `generateText` is called with no `maxTokens` cap at two sites, so a worst-case generation can fill the context budget; repeated tool results aren't cached either.

dimensions.name: Eval / quality gates
dimensions.label: Strong
dimensions.why: A real 84-fixture eval suite runs on every PR via CI — the strongest dimension. Held back from a perfect score only because fixtures assert outputs, not tool selection.

dimensions.name: Safety
dimensions.label: Adequate
dimensions.why: Basic input handling is present and the agent has no destructive tools, but there is no explicit prompt-injection defense on the web content the research tools ingest directly.

dimensions.name: Prompt decomposition
dimensions.label: Weak
dimensions.why: A single ~4,200-token system prompt mixes role, tool notes, output rules, three few-shot examples, and a recurring runbook — all paid for on every turn, even one-line follow-ups.

dimensions.name: Definition quality
dimensions.label: Weak
dimensions.why: 28 of 41 tools say what they do but not when to use them, and six use single-letter parameters with no enums on finite fields — weak for both the model and BM25 retrieval.

category_takeaways.Architecture & orchestration: Solid foundation — a single named agent, clean decomposition, sensible model use. No structural changes needed; spend the effort elsewhere.
category_takeaways.Context & tools: The weak point, and the highest-leverage area. The 41-tool catalog and a ~4,200-token monolithic prompt dominate per-turn cost — see *Tool sprawl*, *Bloated tool descriptions*, *Near-duplicate tools*, and *Monolithic system prompt* below.
category_takeaways.Reliability & safety: In good shape — the eval suite is the standout, but it checks outputs, not tool selection, so sprawl-driven mis-selection slips through (see *Eval suite covers tasks but not tool selection*).
category_takeaways.Operations: Flying blind. Nothing is traced and generations are uncapped — see *No observability wired* and *No max_tokens cap*. Close the tracing loop first; it makes every other recommendation measurable.

findings.title: No observability wired
findings.evidence: No `langfuse`, `langsmith`, `@opentelemetry/*`, `openinference`, `openllmetry`, or `helicone` packages in `package.json`. No telemetry init in `src/api/chat/route.ts`. No tracing env vars in `.env.example`.
findings.rationale: The agent runs in production (`README.md` describes a live customer surface) but there is no way for anyone outside the running process to observe what it does. Regressions across deploys are invisible; cost spikes are invisible; per-tool failure modes are invisible. None of the other findings in this report are independently verifiable without this loop closed.
findings.recommendation: Wire Langfuse via the patterns in `/ratel-observability-assessment` before any other change in this report. Two-day landing window is typical.
findings.ratel_angle: Routes to `/ratel-observability-assessment` — and downstream, once data is flowing, build the cost and retrieval-quality dashboards that will measure the rest of the report's recommendations.

findings.title: Tool sprawl on the chat turn
findings.evidence: `src/agents/research-agent/tools.ts:18-487` registers 41 tools. All are passed in `tools:` on `generateText` at `src/api/chat/route.ts:42`. Average description length 187 tokens; longest is `web_research_brief` at 612 tokens.
findings.rationale: Every chat turn sends all 41 tool descriptions to the model. At ~140 tokens average × 41 tools, the tool catalog alone is ≈5,700 input tokens before any user content or conversation history. This is most of the input cost on a typical turn and a significant fraction of context budget on small models.
findings.recommendation: Pre-filter the tool list per turn so the model only sees the top-K (typically 8) most-relevant tools for the user's message. The full catalog remains addressable via a discovery surface.
findings.ratel_angle: Matches Ratel's BM25 tool retrieval + replace-mode pre-filter (shipped, v0.1.6 line). Expected input-token reduction is 50–85% on the catalog portion of the prompt.

findings.title: Bloated tool descriptions
findings.evidence: `src/agents/research-agent/tools.ts` median description 156 tokens. Five tools (`web_research_brief`, `crawl_pages`, `summarize_findings`, `cite_sources`, `synthesize_report`) have descriptions over 350 tokens each, all with multi-paragraph examples inline.
findings.rationale: Long descriptions inflate the catalog cost (compounding the sprawl finding) and also confuse the model's tool selection — the inline examples often mention other tools, which the model treats as routing hints.
findings.recommendation: Trim descriptions to one short "what it does" sentence + one "when to use" line. Move examples and edge-case detail to a separate spec document the agent does not see every turn.
findings.ratel_angle: Matches Ratel's BM25 retrieval (shipped, v0.1.6 line) — the dashboard surfaces low top-hit scores for confusing descriptions.

findings.title: Near-duplicate tools
findings.evidence: Three pairs flagged — `web_research_brief` vs `crawl_pages`; `summarize_findings` vs `synthesize_report`; `cite_sources` vs `format_citations`. Descriptions overlap >40% by token bag.
findings.rationale: The model picks among near-duplicates inconsistently, which makes the agent's behavior across turns less predictable than it should be. Several open issues in `evals/results/` mention "wrong tool selected" without naming the disambiguation.
findings.recommendation: Consolidate each pair, or rewrite descriptions to be sharply distinguishing ("brief: fast, free-text overview" vs "crawl: deep, structured fetch with page bodies").
findings.ratel_angle: Matches Ratel's BM25 retrieval (shipped, v0.1.6 line) — top-hit-score distribution surfaces the duplication empirically.

findings.title: Monolithic system prompt mixing many responsibilities
findings.evidence: `src/agents/research-agent/prompt.ts:5` is a single ~4,200-token system prompt that mixes role definition, inline tool usage notes, output-format rules, three few-shot examples, and a recurring "research → summarise → cite → synthesize" runbook. The same runbook appears again, near-verbatim, in `scripts/digest.ts:12` and `src/agents/research-agent/summarizer.ts:9`.
findings.rationale: The prompt carries every concern on every turn, so the runbook and examples pay input-token rent even when the turn is a one-line follow-up. The duplicated runbook across three call sites also means a change to the procedure has to be made — and kept consistent — in three places.
findings.recommendation: Keep a lean core prompt (role + output contract + safety) and extract the runbook and examples into named, retrievable skills the agent loads on demand. Deduplicate the three runbook copies into one unit first.
findings.ratel_angle: Matches Ratel's first-class skills (v0.1.6, shipped) — the runbook only enters context when the turn calls for it. Route to `/ratel-decompose-prompt`.

findings.title: Tool descriptions missing "when to use"
findings.evidence: 28 of the 41 tools in `src/agents/research-agent/tools.ts` describe what the tool does but never say when to reach for it; six (`fetch`, `read`, `store`, `lookup`, `query`, `format`) use a single-word `q` or `data` parameter with no enum on finite fields like `mode` and `sort`.
findings.rationale: The model has to infer selection intent from "what it does" alone, and Ratel's BM25 index has fewer terms to match the user's turn against — both the "when to use" clause and descriptive parameter names are part of the index.
findings.recommendation: Rewrite each description as one "what it does" sentence plus a one-line "when to use," rename single-letter parameters, and add enums on the finite-value fields.
findings.ratel_angle: BM25 indexes names + descriptions + parameter names + enum values and strips schema structure (ADR-0004). Route to `/ratel-tune-definitions`.

findings.title: No max_tokens cap on chat-turn generations
findings.rationale: A worst-case generation can fill the model's context budget. On a Sonnet-class model that is a 16K+ token output for a turn that frequently only needs 500 tokens.
findings.recommendation: Cap per call. Sensible defaults: 4096 for chat turns, 1024 for the summarizer step.

findings.title: Anemic prompt versioning
findings.evidence: System prompt is an inline template string in `src/agents/research-agent/prompt.ts:5`. No version constant, no hash, no git-tracked prompt id.
findings.rationale: Prompt regressions across deploys will be undetectable once observability is wired.
findings.recommendation: Extract the prompt to `prompts/research-agent.v1.md` and attach a `prompt_version` constant. Roll the version on every change.

findings.title: Eval suite covers tasks but not tool selection
findings.evidence: `evals/research-agent.jsonl` has 84 labeled tasks with expected outputs but no `expected_tool_id` field. CI gate in `.github/workflows/evals.yml` runs on every PR.
findings.rationale: The eval suite catches output regressions but cannot detect tool-selection regressions (e.g., the agent producing a correct-looking answer using the wrong tool). Given the tool sprawl finding above, this is worth closing.
findings.recommendation: Label `ground_truth_tool_id` (or the equivalent list of acceptable tool ids) on each fixture.
findings.ratel_angle: Matches the Retrieval Quality dashboard's `recall@5` widget (shipped, v0.1.6 line) — once ground truth exists, retrieval quality becomes measurable.

where_ratel_fits:
Three findings in this report (tool sprawl, bloated descriptions, near-duplicates) are the textbook fit for Ratel's shipped v0.1.6 surface: BM25 tool retrieval with replace-mode pre-filter, paired with the Retrieval Quality dashboard. Once observability lands, the dashboard will show the input-token reduction directly and surface low top-hit scores for the descriptions that need rewriting.

The monolithic-prompt finding fits Ratel's first-class skills (v0.1.6, shipped): the duplicated runbook becomes a retrievable skill that Ratel ranks alongside tools and loads on demand, so it stops paying input-token rent every turn. The description-quality finding is the wording side of the same BM25 index — names, descriptions, parameter names, and enum values all feed retrieval (ADR-0004), so tightening them lifts recall directly.
next_steps: `/ratel-observability-assessment` — addresses *No observability wired* (Critical, Observability). Required before any of the below can be measured.

`/ratel-integrate` — addresses *Tool sprawl*, *Bloated tool descriptions*, and *Near-duplicate tools* (all Major, Tool surface). Pilot scope should be the `chat-turn` trace, A/B split via a feature flag.
`/ratel-decompose-prompt` — addresses *Monolithic system prompt* (Major, Prompt decomposition). Extract the duplicated runbook and examples into retrievable skills.
`/ratel-tune-definitions` — addresses *Tool descriptions missing "when to use"* (Major, Definition quality). Rewrite descriptions and tighten parameter names / enums.
appendix:
- Entry points: `src/api/chat/route.ts:42` (single).
- Sub-agents: `src/agents/research-agent/summarizer.ts:14` (called from the main loop; not separately tool-equipped).
- Tools: 41 registered in `src/agents/research-agent/tools.ts`. All used at one call site.
- Prompts: 1 inline template (`src/agents/research-agent/prompt.ts:5`). No versioning.
- Observability: none.
- Evals: 84 fixtures in `evals/research-agent.jsonl`; CI gate in `.github/workflows/evals.yml`.
